summary: ExtractClusters normalizes PeakRank=9999 to 0 at parse time (next to the existing Rank->CurrentRank normalization) so the sentinel does not block a later occurrence's legitimate PeakRank via the first-non-zero-wins merge guard, and so the json:peakRank,omitempty tag drops it from CLI JSON output.
reason: 9999 is Digg's internal placeholder for newly-detected clusters with no tracked all-time peak. Leaking it into CLI JSON forces every downstream agent to special-case the value. Normalizing at parse time (rather than at the output boundary) also prevents a sentinel occurrence from blocking a legitimate later PeakRank under the first-non-zero-wins merge guard introduced in the sibling rank-merge patch.
validated_outcome: TestExtractClustersNormalizesPeakRankSentinel verifies normalization + JSON omission; TestExtractClustersSentinelDoesNotBlockLaterPeakRank verifies the parse-time placement (sentinel-then-real interaction); TestExtractClustersPreservesLegitimatePeakRank confirms non-sentinel values pass through unchanged; TestExtractClustersOmitsAbsentPeakRank confirms absent-field behavior is unchanged.
